editor_settings.title: Best Price Canon G7X Mark III Right Now May 2026: What Counts As A Good Deal?
editor_settings.content:
Best Price Canon G7X Mark III Right Now May 2026: What Counts As A Good Deal?

The best Canon G7X Mark III price right now is not one number.

It depends on condition.

But if you want the simple answer, the best realistic deal in May 2026 is refurbished under $704 or new at MSRP around $849.99.

The Best Price Targets

Here is what I would consider a good buy right now:

  • Best possible refurbished price: $679.99
  • Still good refurbished price: $703.99
  • Good new retail price: $849.99
  • Fair used/open-box range: $750 to $950
  • Common marked-up market range: $1,175 to $1,299

That last range is where I would be careful.

It is available more often, but it is not the best value.

Why The Price Is Still High

The G7X Mark III should be a normal compact camera by now.

It came out years ago, but it keeps getting pushed by social media because it gives people the small-camera look without carrying a big mirrorless setup.

The flip screen, 4K video, fast lens, pocket size, and Canon color all help.

Then the hype creates a loop. More videos make more people search for it. More searches empty retail stock. Empty stock pushes buyers to resellers.

That is how an $849.99 camera starts showing up at $1,200.

The Deal I Would Take Fast

If you find a refurbished G7X Mark III near $679.99, I would not overthink it.

That is the strongest price in this market.

If you find a new one at $849.99 from Canon, B&H, Adorama, Best Buy, or another authorized retailer, that is also a good buy.

It may not feel cheap, but it is much better than paying reseller pricing.

The Deal I Would Avoid

I would avoid paying $1,175 to $1,299 for a regular black or silver G7X Mark III unless you need it immediately.

That price range makes more sense for scarcity than value.

The 30th Anniversary Graphite Edition is different because it has a special finish and limited-edition appeal. Even then, if resale climbs over $1,500, you are buying collector energy more than camera value.

Check This Before Paying Markup

Because the big retailers sell out so fast, smaller live listings can sometimes be the better move.

This is the kind of listing I would check before paying a marketplace seller over $1,000.

[product-card:canon-powershot-g7x-mark-iii-202mp-4k-digital-camera-42x-optical-zoom-98-new]


Final Verdict

The best Canon G7X Mark III price right now is refurbished around $679.99.

The best new price is MSRP around $849.99.

The best used price is anything clean under $850.

If the only listings you see are $1,200 or higher, wait if you can.

This camera is worth buying at the right price. It is not worth letting social media pressure you into the worst price.
